data.title: U.S. Treasury to Release AI Resources for Financial System Security
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department announced plans to release a set of six resources in February, developed in collaboration with industry and federal and state regulatory partners. According to Jin10, these resources aim to ensure the safety and resilience of artificial intelligence within the U.S. financial system. The initiative underscores the growing importance of AI in financial operations and the need for robust frameworks to manage associated risks. The Treasury's efforts reflect a proactive approach to integrating AI technologies while safeguarding financial stability.

data.title: European Leaders Face Key Decision Amid French Election Concerns
data.description: European leaders are poised to make a significant decision regarding one of the region's most influential positions ahead of the potential for a far-right victory in the French elections next year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the substantial opportunity this presents, along with the considerable political risks involved. The timing of this decision is crucial as it could impact the political landscape in Europe, especially with the looming possibility of a shift in France's political direction. The outcome of this decision could have far-reaching implications for the region's stability and future political dynamics.

data.title: Canada and Czechia Head to Overtime in Men's Hockey Quarterfinals at 2026 Winter Olympics
data.description: Canada, a leading contender in the men's hockey tournament at the 2026 Winter Olympics, has advanced to overtime against Czechia after leveling the score in the final minutes of regulation. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the intense competition as both teams vie for a spot in the semifinals. The match has captivated audiences, showcasing the skill and determination of both squads as they battle for Olympic glory.

data.title: Indian University Asked to Leave AI Summit Over Allegations
data.description: An Indian university was requested to vacate its exhibition booth at the India AI Impact Summit following accusations of passing off work as its own. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the incident that led to the university's removal from the event. The allegations centered around the university's presentation, which was claimed to be misrepresented as original work. The summit, focused on advancements in artificial intelligence, saw various participants showcasing their innovations and contributions to the field. The university's departure from the summit underscores the importance of authenticity and integrity in academic and technological exhibitions.

data.title: Apex Group Partners with WLFI to Pilot USD1 in Tokenized Fund System
data.description: Apex Group, which manages $3.5 trillion in assets, has announced a partnership with WLFI, the Trump family's decentralized finance (DeFi) project, to test USD1 as a payment method for fund transactions within Apex's tokenized fund system. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration was revealed at the World Liberty Forum held in Mar-a-Lago. Apex is also considering the possibility of listing WLFI's tokenized assets on the London Stock Exchange Group's digital platform. Additionally, WLFI is planning to introduce an app that will connect bank accounts with digital wallets.

data.title: Ivory Coast Secures $1.3 Billion in Eurobonds Amid Favorable Market Conditions
data.description: Ivory Coast successfully raised $1.3 billion through eurobonds on Wednesday, aiming to support its budgetary needs. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that African countries are taking advantage of reduced borrowing costs and a positive investor outlook to access financial markets. This move reflects a broader trend among African nations seeking to capitalize on favorable economic conditions to secure funding for various fiscal requirements. The issuance of eurobonds by Ivory Coast is part of a strategic approach to manage its financial obligations and stimulate economic growth.

data.title: Zora Protocol Migrates to Solana for Enhanced Scalability and Lower Fees
data.description:
The Zora protocol has announced its decision to migrate from the Ethereum blockchain to Solana, aiming to benefit from improved scalability and reduced transaction fees. According to NS3.AI, this move is part of Zora's strategy to enhance its operational efficiency and user experience.

In other developments, the Ethereum Foundation is undergoing a leadership change as Stańczak steps down from the role of Executive Director. This transition marks a significant shift within the organization, potentially impacting its future direction.

Additionally, the blockchain space is witnessing new initiatives, including the launch of a member-only decentralized exchange on the XRPL platform. This development is expected to offer exclusive trading opportunities for its members.

Furthermore, Ethereum is experiencing renewed activity in the decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) sector, indicating a resurgence of interest and innovation in this area.

data.title: Mark Zuckerberg to Testify in Social Media Addiction Trial
data.description: Mark Zuckerberg appeared at a Los Angeles court ahead of his scheduled testimony on Wednesday in a significant trial concerning social media addiction. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the focus on how Meta Platforms Inc. manages the balance between safeguarding young users and increasing revenue. The trial is expected to delve into the company's practices and policies regarding user protection and its financial strategies. Zuckerberg's testimony is anticipated to shed light on Meta's approach to these critical issues.

data.title: Bitcoin 2024 buyers steady BTC price as trader sees $52K 'next week or so'
data.description:
Two-year Bitcoin hodlers "absorbed" seller pressure in recent weeks, according to new research, but most analysts still expect new macro BTC price lows.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-early-2024-buyers-steady-btc-price-as-trader-sees-52k-next-week?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: CFTC Clash Deepens as Nevada Targets Kalshi
data.description:
Key Insights Nevada sued Kalshi on Tuesday after the Ninth Circuit denied the company’s bid to block state enforcement over sports prediction markets. The ruling allowed the Nevada Gaming Control Board to file a civil action in state court alleging unlicensed wagering.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2026/02/18/cftc-clash-deepens-as-nevada-targets-kalshi/

data.title: President Trump Signs the Largest Tax Cuts in U.S. History
data.description:
President Donald J. Trump signed The One Big Beautiful Bill into law, enacting what the White House describes as the largest tax cuts in American history. Including provisions that eliminate federal income tax on tips, overtime pay, and Social Security income for most taxpayers. The law was formally signed on July 4, 2025, and the […] The post President Trump Signs the Largest Tax Cuts in U
source: https://techgaged.com/president-trump-signs-the-largest-tax-cuts-in-u-s-history/

data.title: Eric Trump Says He’s Never Been More Bullish on Bitcoin, and That Alone Matters
data.description:
Eric Trump recently stated that he has never felt more optimistic about Bitcoin than he does now, emphasizing that this declaration carries significant weight and relevance in today’s financial landscape. His confidence in the cryptocurrency market underscores his belief in Bitcoin's potential for growth and stability, and his prominent position as a public figure adds to the importance of his statement. This level of enthusiasm from someone of his stature may influence public perception and investor sentiment toward Bitcoin, making his opinion a noteworthy factor in discussions about the future of digital currencies.
source: https://blocknews.com/eric-trump-says-hes-never-been-more-bullish-on-bitcoin-and-that-alone-matters/

data.title: Isabel Schnabel Affirms Commitment to ECB Role
data.description: Isabel Schnabel has confirmed her dedication to completing her term on the European Central Bank's Executive Board. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Schnabel's statement amid ongoing discussions about the ECB's future direction. Schnabel's role is crucial as the ECB navigates economic challenges and policy decisions. Her commitment comes at a time when the bank is under scrutiny for its monetary strategies and economic impact. Schnabel's assurance aims to provide stability and continuity within the ECB's leadership.

data.title: Fed's Inflation Expectations Index Shows Sub-2% Levels
data.description:
The Federal Reserve's inflation expectations index has indicated levels below 2%, suggesting a potential easing in inflation pressures. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X that this development could influence future monetary policy decisions. The index, which measures consumer expectations for inflation, has been closely monitored by economists and policymakers.

The sub-2% reading is significant as it aligns with the Federal Reserve's long-term inflation target, potentially impacting interest rate decisions. Analysts are considering how this data might affect the central bank's approach to managing economic growth and inflation.

While the index provides a snapshot of consumer sentiment, it is one of many indicators used by the Federal Reserve to gauge economic conditions. The current reading may prompt discussions on whether adjustments to interest rates are necessary to maintain economic stability.

Overall, the sub-2% inflation expectation reflects a period of relative stability in consumer prices, which could have broader implications for economic policy and financial markets.
